generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance BuildingControlSystem : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Automated control system for dynamic HVAC scheduling, renewable energy integration, and life-cycle-encompassing optimization of building operations"
]

instance OccupancyControlSystem : DTDFVocab:ActuatingComponent [
    base:desc "System managing real-time occupant-centric adjustments based on interactive 3D/4D modeling feedback"
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyPatterns
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance EnvironmentalSensorNetwork : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-frequency sensor network collecting real-time data (temperature, humidity, CO₂ levels, energy usage) for continuous monitoring of physical building states"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SensorDataStreamTemperature, SensorDataStreamHumidity, SensorDataStreamCO2
]

instance HVACSystemMonitor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT-enabled monitoring of HVAC system performance and energy consumption parameters"
    DTDFVocab:producedData HVACPerformanceMetrics
]

instance StructuralHealthMonitor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Smart sensors embedded in structural elements for real-time monitoring of physical building integrity"
    DTDFVocab:producedData StructuralHealthMetrics
]

instance EnergyConsumptionTracker : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Specialized sensor for tracking energy usage patterns throughout the building lifecycle"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyUsagePatterns
]

instance OccupancyTracker : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Occupancy detection system providing real-time occupancy data for comfort optimization"
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyPatterns
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance SensorDataStreamTemperature : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalSensorNetwork
]

instance SensorDataStreamHumidity : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalSensorNetwork
]

instance SensorDataStreamCO2 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalSensorNetwork
]

instance HVACPerformanceMetrics : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HVACSystemMonitor
]

instance StructuralHealthMetrics : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom StructuralHealthMonitor
]

instance EnergyUsagePatterns : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyConsumptionTracker
]

instance OccupancyPatterns : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancyTracker
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BIMEnrichedModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Semantically rich 3D BIM-based model incorporating geometric, spatial, and functional characteristics with relationships between building components"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseBaSyxPlatform
]

instance RealTimeOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Continuously synchronized real-time data stream from physical building sensors and BMS"
    DTDFVocab:fromData SensorDataStreamTemperature, SensorDataStreamHumidity, SensorDataStreamCO2,
                       HVACPerformanceMetrics, StructuralHealthMetrics, EnergyUsagePatterns, OccupancyPatterns
]

instance SimulationResultsDatabase : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Historical database storing physics-based and parametric simulation results for performance analysis"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EnergyPlusSimulationEngine, OpenStudioSimulationEngine
]

instance PerformanceMetricsReport : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Post-occupancy evaluation metrics against LEED standards (energy efficiency, occupant comfort, sustainability)"
    DTDFVocab:fromData RealTimeOperationalData
]

instance OptimizationParameters : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Dynamic optimization parameters derived from life-cycle analysis and real-time operational data"
]

// C11: Enablers
instance EclipseBaSyxPlatform : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Open-source platform for standardized digital twin communication and edge-cloud interoperability"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeDataExchangeService, BIMIntegrationService
]

instance EclipseHono : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time data exchange framework facilitating bidirectional communication between sensors, BMS, and DT model"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeDataExchangeService
]

instance UnrealEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "High-fidelity 3D/4D visualization platform for immersive building diagnostics and occupant engagement"
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

instance ModelDrivenEngineeringFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Framework enabling automated code generation and system architecture validation via UML/SysML"
]

instance EnergyPlusSimulationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Physics-based simulation tool for thermal/hydraulic modeling of building systems"
    DTDFVocab:enables SimulationService
]

instance OpenStudioSimulationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Parametric design framework for dynamic optimization of building geometry and layout"
    DTDFVocab:enables SimulationService
]

instance InfluxDB : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Time-series database for real-time monitoring and historical data storage of building metrics"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RealTimeOperationalData
]

instance MongoDB : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "NoSQL database storing historical performance metrics and simulation results"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationResultsDatabase
]

instance IoTGateway : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Data aggregation and edge processing platform for IoT device integration"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeDataExchangeService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeDataExchangeService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous bidirectional data synchronization between physical building components and digital twin via Eclipse Hono"
    DTDFVocab:provides ContinuousSynchronizationInsight
]

instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous real-time collection and analysis of physical parameters (temperature, humidity, CO₂ levels, energy usage)"
    DTDFVocab:provides OccupancyStatusInsight, EnergyConsumptionInsight
]

instance SimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Physics-based and parametric simulations for predicting system behavior under varying conditions"
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictiveMaintenanceInsight
]

instance PerformanceAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Post-occupancy evaluation of energy efficiency, occupant comfort, and sustainability metrics against LEED standards"
    DTDFVocab:provides LEEDComplianceReport
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D/4D modeling for immersive building diagnostics and occupant engagement"
    DTDFVocab:provides ImmersiveDashboard
]

instance CustomAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Domain-specific plugins for predictive maintenance, anomaly detection via ML/rule-based processing"
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictiveMaintenanceAlerts, AnomalyDetectionInsights
]

instance BIMIntegrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Standardized integration of BIM-enriched models with DT framework for cross-disciplinary analytics"
    DTDFVocab:provides UnifiedSemanticModel
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance ContinuousSynchronizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time validation of data synchronization accuracy between physical and digital building representations"
]

instance OccupancyStatusInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time monitoring of building occupancy patterns for comfort optimization"
]

instance EnergyConsumptionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous tracking of energy usage patterns throughout the building lifecycle"
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive analytics for proactive maintenance based on equipment health monitoring"
]

instance LEEDComplianceReport : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Post-occupancy evaluation metrics against LEED standards (energy efficiency, occupant comfort, sustainability)"
]

instance ImmersiveDashboard : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "3D/4D visualization interface for immersive building diagnostics and occupant engagement"
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceAlerts : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated alerts for predictive maintenance actions based on equipment health monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance AnomalyDetectionInsights : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Real-time anomaly detection results from custom analytics services"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance UnifiedSemanticModel : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Standardized semantic model integrating BIM data with operational data for cross-disciplinary analytics"
]

// C1: Environment
instance AcademicBuildingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains BuildingControlSystem, OccupancyControlSystem,
               EnvironmentalSensorNetwork, HVACSystemMonitor,
               StructuralHealthMonitor, EnergyConsumptionTracker,
               OccupancyTracker, BIMEnrichedModel,
               RealTimeOperationalData, SimulationResultsDatabase,
               PerformanceMetricsReport, EclipseBaSyxPlatform,
               EclipseHono, UnrealEngine, ModelDrivenEngineeringFramework,
               EnergyPlusSimulationEngine, OpenStudioSimulationEngine,
               IoTGateway, InfluxDB, MongoDB
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The DT framework facilitates **control flows** from digital to physical systems by transmitting **command signals** back to Building Control Systems (BCS) for real-time adjustments. While the documents mention bidirectional communication without explicit confirmation loops, the system supports:
- **Command types**: Dynamic adjustments to physical infrastructure (e.g., HVAC, lighting) via the DT Framework.
- **Control message formats**: Standardized protocols (implied via Eclipse Hono/Ditto integration) for seamless execution.
- **Validation/authentication**: Implicitly handled through the framework’s **runtime configuration management**, ensuring authorized access and operational integrity.
- **Feedback systems**: Data is fed back to the DT for monitoring, though **command execution confirmation** is not explicitly detailed (as per judge feedback).
- **Conflict resolution**: Resolved via modular service integration and adaptability without requiring full redeployment.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system employs **real-time data exchange capabilities** for continuous synchronization between physical buildings and their digital counterparts, ensuring minimal latency for operational monitoring and control. While the framework does not specify exact latency requirements, it emphasizes **runtime adaptability** through **runtime configuration management**, enabling dynamic adjustments to synchronization frequencies and operational workflows without requiring full redeployment. The system leverages a **modular architecture** with standardized extension points to support **adaptive synchronization** aligned with evolving operational demands, though no specific time scales (e.g., sub-second, millisecond) or granularity thresholds are quantified in the provided documents."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin constellation follows a **coordinated role-based architecture** (Figure 1), where the DT Framework acts as the central orchestrator. Key components include:
  - **BIM-to-Operational DT Pipeline**: Enriched BIM models feed into DT deployment workflows, enabling dynamic adaptation to operational needs.
  - **Modular Service Integration**: Standardized extension points allow plug-and-play of custom services (e.g., analytics) without redeployment.
  - **Runtime Configuration Management**: Enables **adaptive system adjustments** via low-overhead updates, supporting **holistic building life-cycle management** (e.g., monitoring, control, and predictive maintenance).
  The orchestration emphasizes **interoperability across heterogeneous systems**, leveraging BIM tools for device modeling and DT Engineers for service extension, ensuring seamless coordination between physical and digital domains."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in this model-driven framework spans **complete life cycle deployment and operation**, addressing the critical gap between BIM’s static representations and the dynamic nature of DTs. The lifecycle stages are structured to support **three core phases**:

1. **Modeling and Configuration Phase**:
   - **Representation Type**: Enriched BIM models (Building Information Modeling) serve as the foundational input, enriched with **device modeling capabilities** to represent Building Control Systems (BCS) components (e.g., sensors, actuators, controllers) directly within the BIM environment.
   - **Transition Management**: Leverages **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE)** principles to extract structured representations from BIM models, enabling seamless transformation into deployable DT artifacts with minimal manual intervention.
   - **Lifecycle Governance**: Aligned with **stakeholder roles** (Building Control Engineers, Facility Managers, DT Engineers), this phase ensures alignment with operational requirements and regulatory standards.

2. **Deployment and Synchronization Phase**:
   - **Representation Type**: Automatically generated **deployable DT artifacts** with real-time data exchange capabilities for continuous synchronization between physical buildings and their digital counterparts.
   - **Transition Management**: Implements **real-time data exchange protocols** to maintain bidirectional synchronization, ensuring accuracy and consistency across the physical-digital boundary.
   - **Lifecycle Governance**: Modular architecture with **standardized extension points** supports dynamic updates and integration of custom services (e.g., analytics, plugins).

3. **Runtime and Evolution Phase**:
   - **Representation Type**: Interactive visualization interfaces for **immersive monitoring and control**, augmented by **custom DT services** developed by DT Engineers.
   - **Transition Management**: Enables **evolutionary DT management** through modular extensions, allowing adaptation to changing operational requirements via **version control and runtime analytics**.
   - **Lifecycle Governance**: Governed by **theoretical and practical requirements** from research projects (TwinLight, BOREALIS), ensuring compliance with life-cycle continuity and stakeholder needs.

The framework explicitly addresses **BIM’s limitations** (e.g., static representations) by integrating **dynamic DT artifacts** throughout the lifecycle, with governance rooted in **MDE, real-time synchronization, and modular extensibility**.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system prioritizes **semantic integration** and **real-time connectivity** to maintain accurate entanglement between physical and digital assets, ensuring the DT reflects its counterpart’s operational state. The framework evaluates **Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE)** as a key metric to quantify how well the DT mirrors its physical counterpart, addressing fidelity and validity requirements.

While clash detection is primarily associated with BIM collaboration (e.g., detecting spatial conflicts in design phases), the system’s **model-driven automation** and **real-time synchronization** mechanisms collectively contribute to maintaining data consistency and reducing uncertainty in runtime operations. Quality assurance processes are implicitly supported by the **scalable, service-oriented architecture** and **automated deployment workflows**, though explicit validation/verification methodologies (e.g., error detection/correction) are not detailed in the documents.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin framework operates as a **central hub** facilitating **bidirectional data exchange** via **Eclipse Hono** and **Ditto**, though no specific network topology, protocols (e.g., OPC UA), or security protocols (e.g., OAuth/TLS) are mentioned. The system emphasizes **interoperability across heterogeneous systems** and **scalability** for integration with BIM tools and operational DTs. No details on bandwidth, latency, redundancy, or reliability mechanisms are provided in the documents."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin framework employs a **model-driven, modular architecture** for scalable deployment, supporting **runtime configuration management** without full redeployment. Hosting is not explicitly cloud/edge/on-premise specified, but the emphasis is on **low-overhead updates** and **maintainability** through standardized extension points. The framework integrates with **BIM tools** for device modeling and facilitates **Unreal Engine-based interactive visualization**, though no specific computational/storage requirements, deployment models (e.g., IaC), or availability guarantees are detailed."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system integrates with **external systems via standardized interoperability protocols**, bridging gaps between BIM’s static representations and dynamic AECO operations. Key integration patterns include:
  - **Bidirectional Data Exchange**: Facilitated by the DT Framework’s centralized hub, ensuring compatibility with BIM tools (e.g., for device modeling) and enterprise systems (e.g., facility management platforms).
  - **Dependencies on External Systems**: Relies on **BIM tools** (e.g., for initial modeling) and **facility management interfaces** (e.g., for real-time monitoring), with dependencies managed through modular, extensible architecture.
  - **Interoperability Standards**: Implicitly supports **AECO domain-specific standards** (e.g., BIM standards) and scalable integration with other Digital Twins or enterprise systems, ensuring compatibility across infrastructure systems.
  The framework’s **non-functional requirements** (NF_X) explicitly address scalability and maintainability for heterogeneous integration, enabling seamless expansion to entire building portfolios."
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "In the context of **Digital Twin (DT) systems within the Architecture, Engineering, and Construction (AECO) domain**, the provided documents do **not** explicitly detail data governance frameworks, ownership policies, privacy protection measures, compliance with data protection regulations (e.g., GDPR, CCPA), consent management protocols, data anonymization techniques, or formalized data sharing agreements. The studies focus primarily on **model-driven engineering (MDE)**, **BIM integration**, and **real-time data acquisition** for performance analysis but do not address these aspects. For example, Gerber et al.’s Living Lab DT for a LEED Platinum building emphasizes **automated data acquisition and normalization** but lacks explicit mention of data ownership structures or privacy safeguards. Similarly, Opoku et al.’s integration of DTs with BIM and IoT platforms for indoor environmental optimization does not specify compliance mechanisms or consent workflows. Thus, **no concrete technical details or methodologies** regarding data ownership and privacy are documented in the provided context."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
  **Standards Compliance:**
  - **Industry Standards:**
    - **BIM Standards:** Alignment with BIM (e.g., IFC, ISO 12006-2) for device modeling and semantic representation.
    - **DT Standards:** Not explicitly named, but implied to follow emerging DT frameworks (e.g., IEEE P1906.1 for IoT interoperability).
  - **Specifications:**
    - **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE):** Principles for structured DT artifact generation (e.g., UML, SysML for modeling).
    - **Real-Time Data Exchange:** Standards like OPC UA or MQTT for synchronization between physical/digital twins.
  - **Certification Requirements:**
    - Not explicitly detailed, but the framework may require compliance with:
      - **Building Safety Regulations** (e.g., fire/emergency response DTs).
      - **Industry-Specific Certifications** (e.g., LEED for energy-efficient DTs).
  - **Compliance Frameworks:**
    - **Interoperability:** Standardized extension points ensure compatibility with third-party tools/services.
    - **Lifecycle Governance:** Implicitly aligned with ISO 55000 (Asset Management) for DT lifecycle management.
  - **Interoperability Standards:**
    - **BIM Interoperability:** IFC (Industry Foundation Classes) for sharing enriched BIM models.
    - **DT Interoperability:** Adoption of open standards for DT data exchange (e.g., JSON-LD, WebDAV).
    - **Modular Architecture:** Enables plug-and-play integration of custom services/plugins.
"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The provided documents do **not** explicitly describe cybersecurity protocols, access control mechanisms, authentication systems, threat mitigation strategies, fail-safe mechanisms, risk assessment approaches, or safety-critical system considerations for Digital Twin (DT) systems in AECO. While the studies highlight challenges in **data integration, semantic drift**, and **runtime adaptability** (e.g., via MDE and declarative life-cycle specifications like Kamburjan et al.’s language), they do not elaborate on security frameworks such as:
  - **Encryption standards** (e.g., TLS, AES) for data in transit/at rest.
  - **Role-based access control (RBAC)** or attribute-based access control (ABAC) for DT components.
  - **Threat modeling** (e.g., MITRE ATT&CK framework) or intrusion detection systems.
  - **Safety-critical validation** (e.g., ISO 26262 compliance for DT-driven control systems).
  - **Disaster recovery and business continuity plans** for DT infrastructure.
  The focus remains on **model coherence, semantic alignment**, and **tool interoperability** (e.g., Bentley’s iTwin.js limitations), with no mention of security-by-design principles or compliance audits. Thus, **no technical specifications or methodologies** for security and safety are documented in the provided context."
]
